data.body:
WS3 design + advisory triage (plan-review PASSED round-1, signed; 13 advisories baked into implementation).

KEY DESIGN DECISION (after tracing the engine): the v3 'batch' runners do NOT run distinct standalone per-criterion prompts with a diff context — DefaultBatchRunner runs ONE finder over criteria-as-data; ProductionBatchRunner is plan-review-bound (drives run_pass1 over registry.by_id()); criterion with: is passed raw (interpreter doesn't resolve it) and a BatchRunner can't read step outputs. So WS3 adds a gate-specific CodeReviewBatchRunner (the parallel to ProductionBatchRunner) constructed WITH the diff context; it runs each INCLUDED overlay prompt (mode=structured, output_schema=code_review_findings) and aggregates findings. The 'batch' construct still provides the membership/when: gating + included/skipped journaling the AC tests. Two rounds: round_a gated by triggers (glob), round_b gated by union (escalation, minus already-run). The diff context is computed once (assemble_diff op) and passed as a workflow input + to the batch runner at construction (WS4 dispatch wires the real path; the offline test constructs it directly).

Advisory triage (all non-blocking): A1/A2/A3 (offline FakeRunner can't emit recommend_overlays) -> the offline test uses a structured-capable fake runner returning canned base output {findings, recommend_overlays} to drive round_b membership. A4/A5 ('glob' operand undefined) -> glob = registry.glob_triggered_overlays(changed_files) (applies_to globs from criteria_routing.json, fnmatch). A6 (merge_findings clustering) -> clusters kernel findings by (location-path, primary-criterion, line-proximity), reusing aggregate.py's _LINE_BUCKET idea (kernel findings use location/criteria, not citations/dimension, so the clustering is adapted not a direct aggregate_findings call). A7 (one-hop) -> structural: round_b overlays output code_review_findings (no recommend_overlays field) + overlay_union runs once. A8/A9 (ADR + config schema doc) -> ADR docs/adr/0010-code-review-escalation.md + the gate config schema documented there + in the yaml header. A11 (overlay_union fallback) -> already_run/recommend default to [] (triggers call passes neither). A12 (workflow_ops.py path) -> ops in code_review/workflow_ops.py, registered via workflow/steps.py import.

NOTE scope: WS3 also adds assemble_diff + code_review_decide + code_review_coach ops (the Pass-2/3/4 wiring the gate yaml needs, parallel to plan_review/workflow_ops.py); WS4 adds produce_code_review_verdict on top. The two NOVEL ops the ticket names are overlay_union + merge_findings.
